def: Smallest deployable unit. One or more containers that share network namespace (same IP, localhost) and can share storage volumes.
A worker machine (physical, VM, or cloud instance) that runs pods. Hosts kubelet, kube-proxy, and a container runtime.
The brains of the cluster: kube-apiserver, etcd, kube-scheduler, kube-controller-manager, cloud-controller-manager.
Front-end of the control plane. All communication with cluster state passes through it — clients, controllers, kubelets.
Distributed, consistent, highly-available key-value store holding all cluster state. The ONE thing you must back up.
Assigns newly created pods to nodes based on resource requests, affinity, taints/tolerations, and other constraints.
Runs controller loops that reconcile actual state toward desired state (node, replication, endpoint, service account, etc.).
Embeds cloud-provider-specific control logic (load balancers, node lifecycle, routes) so core K8s stays cloud-agnostic.
Agent on every node. Watches API server for pods assigned to it and ensures their containers run and stay healthy.
Maintains node network rules (iptables / IPVS / nftables) that implement the Service abstraction.
Manages ReplicaSets; provides rolling updates and rollbacks for stateless applications.
Ensures a specified number of identical pod replicas are running. Usually managed by a Deployment.
Manages pods that need stable, predictable network identities (web-0, web-1) and per-replica persistent storage.
Runs one pod per node (or per matching subset). For node-level agents: log shippers, metrics exporters, CNI plugins.
Runs pods to completion. For batch / one-shot tasks.
Creates Jobs on a cron-style schedule.
Stable network endpoint for a set of pods. Types: ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName, Headless.
Default Service type. Reachable only from within the cluster on an internal virtual IP.
Exposes the Service on a static port (30000-32767) of every node's IP.
Provisions an external load balancer (typically cloud-provided) in front of the Service.
HTTP/HTTPS routing rules from outside the cluster to internal Services. Needs an Ingress controller (nginx, Traefik, etc.) to work.
Stores non-sensitive configuration as key/value pairs. Mountable as files or env vars.
Stores sensitive data (passwords, tokens, keys). Base64-encoded in etcd; encryption-at-rest is configurable.
A cluster resource representing physical storage, provisioned statically or dynamically.
A user's request for storage. Binds to a matching PV, abstracting the storage backend from the pod author.
Describes a class of storage and enables dynamic PV provisioning (e.g., aws-ebs-gp3, csi-cephfs).
A virtual cluster within a cluster. Scope for names and quotas. NOT a security boundary on its own.
Key/value metadata attached to objects. The primary mechanism for identifying and grouping resources.
Query expression matching labels. Used by Services, Deployments, NetworkPolicies, etc.
Non-identifying metadata. For tool-specific info (e.g., kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration).
Role-Based Access Control. Roles define permissions; Bindings grant them to users, groups, or ServiceAccounts.
Identity for processes inside pods. Used for authN against the API server.
Node property that repels pods unless those pods carry a matching toleration.
Pod property allowing scheduling onto a tainted node.
Rules that attract pods to nodes with certain labels (soft or hard preferences).
Detects if a container is still alive. Failure triggers a restart.
Detects if a container is ready for traffic. Failure removes the pod from Service endpoints (no restart).
Detects if a slow-starting app has finished initialization. Disables liveness/readiness until it passes.
Requests = guaranteed floor, used for scheduling. Limits = enforced ceiling. Missing limits = uncapped usage.
Horizontal Pod Autoscaler. Scales replica count based on CPU, memory, or custom/external metrics.
Container Runtime Interface. gRPC API between kubelet and container runtimes.
Container Network Interface. Plugin spec for pod networking. One plugin per cluster (Calico, Cilium, Flannel, Weave).
Container Storage Interface. Lets storage vendors ship drivers without modifying Kubernetes core.
Open Container Initiative. Defines image-spec, runtime-spec, and distribution-spec. The reason images are portable.
CNCF graduated container runtime. Manages image lifecycle and execution. Used by Docker, directly by K8s via CRI.
Lightweight CRI-compliant container runtime, built specifically for Kubernetes.
Default DNS server for service discovery in Kubernetes. Resolves svc.ns.svc.cluster.local records.
K8s resource defining pod-to-pod traffic rules. Enforced by the CNI — silently ignored by CNIs that do not support it.
Three policy levels: Privileged, Baseline, Restricted. Modes: enforce, audit, warn. Replaces PSP.
Runs after authN/authZ, before persistence. Can validate or mutate requests. Webhooks enable custom policy.
Scales the cluster itself — adds nodes for unschedulable pods, removes underutilized ones.
Vertical Pod Autoscaler. Adjusts pod resource requests based on observed usage. Usually not combined with HPA on CPU.
Kubernetes Event-Driven Autoscaler (CNCF). Scales based on external events and queues (Kafka, RabbitMQ, etc.).
Building and running scalable apps in dynamic environments. Techniques: containers, microservices, declarative APIs, immutable infra.
Architecture style: suite of small, independently deployable services, communicating over network.
Methodology for SaaS. Emphasizes declarative setup, env-based config, stateless processes, logs as streams.
Developer deploys code; platform handles infra and scales (often to zero). Pay per execution.
Kubernetes-native serverless platform. Provides scale-to-zero, eventing, and simplified deploy.
Dedicated infra layer for service-to-service comms via sidecar proxies. Handles mTLS, retries, observability.
Popular service mesh built on Envoy. Feature-rich; more complex operational footprint.
CNCF graduated service mesh. Rust-based, lightweight, focused on simplicity.
High-performance L7 proxy. Data plane for Istio, Consul, and many API gateways. CNCF graduated.
Site Reliability Engineering. Treats ops as a software problem. Uses SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets.
SLI = measured signal. SLO = internal target. SLA = external contract with penalties. Error budget = 100% − SLO.
Operational framework for managing and attributing cloud spend. OpenCost is the CNCF project in this space.
Replace components rather than modify in place. Containers naturally fit this model.
Pattern that stops calling a failing dependency to prevent cascading failures.
Metrics (numeric time-series), Logs (timestamped events), Traces (request paths across services).
CNCF graduated. Pull-based metrics monitoring and alerting with a time-series DB and PromQL query language.
Visualization layer. Dashboards for metrics (Prometheus), logs (Loki), and traces (Tempo, Jaeger).
CNCF. Vendor-neutral instrumentation framework for metrics, logs, and traces. Decouples apps from observability backends.
CNCF graduated distributed tracing system. Originated at Uber.
CNCF log collectors. Fluentd is feature-rich; Fluent Bit is lightweight (C), often used as a DaemonSet shipper.
Log aggregation by Grafana Labs. Like Prometheus, but for logs — indexes labels, not full text.
Continuous Integration = frequent merges with automated tests. Continuous Delivery/Deployment = automated release pipeline.
Git as single source of truth; an operator (Argo CD, Flux) reconciles cluster state to match. PRs drive change.
CNCF GitOps CD tool. Strong web UI, app-centric model, popular in enterprise.
CNCF GitOps toolkit. Composable controllers, strong multi-tenancy story.
Kubernetes package manager. Charts are templated, parameterized manifest bundles. CNCF graduated.
Declarative K8s config via bases and overlays (no templates). Built into kubectl via -k flag.
CNCF. Kubernetes-native CI/CD framework. Pipelines, Tasks, and Runs are CRDs; pipeline steps run as pods.
Two identical environments. Switch traffic from old (blue) to new (green) at once; rollback = switch back.
Shift a small percentage of traffic to the new version; increase if healthy, roll back if not.
